action: Built named agents with distinct specialties to create a personal feedback and growth loop.
verbatim: I didn't take her or him or whatever pronoun for Signal as a ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” like, you tell me what to do.
job_to_be_done: Create an external feedback mechanism the work environment does not provide.
subject: self

action: Recognised through Signal that a structural communication pattern had been limiting her impact.
verbatim: There is something in me that is always about bringing the context before talking about impact ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” and I needed to reverse that.
job_to_be_done: Use AI as a mirror to surface invisible communication habits.
subject: self

action: Deployed a writing agent to develop a publishing practice and push ideas into the world.
verbatim: I just need to say to someone: can we have this conversation? And basically refining my conversation with myself, leveraging the mirroring effect of the agent.
job_to_be_done: Overcome writing resistance and build a consistent publishing practice in a second language.
subject: self
open_question: Will this practice hold over time ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” or is the energy of starting enough to sustain it?

action: Built a dedicated SEO agent to accelerate content production and self-coach on a new skill.
verbatim: I noticed that it changed the way I was getting this help ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” it also helped me grow on my understanding.
job_to_be_done: Grow SEO skills fast enough to own a new function without it being a core identity.
subject: self

action: Discovered that the SEO agent could only accelerate knowledge already held, not generate new expertise.
verbatim: I feel ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” or I experienced ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” that you can use a tool only if you know yourself first.
job_to_be_done: Understand where AI amplification ends and self-knowledge begins.
subject: self
open_question: What is the actual ceiling of AI-assisted skill acquisition on topics where baseline knowledge is thin?

action: Discovered critical gaps in SEO infrastructure ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” unindexed pages, missing LLM file, outdated sitemap ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” that no AI tool had flagged.
verbatim: No tool could tell me that, because the tool could only help me identify what I already asked, or what I already knew.
job_to_be_done: Understand the blind spots AI creates when used without full domain context.
subject: self

action: Consciously shifted into a creative director mode ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” asking AI for three options and a recommendation rather than doing the task herself.
verbatim: I'm like: okay, just give me three propositions. And if this is something I need to do on a lot of items, I'm like: give me three options, recommend me one and say why.
job_to_be_done: Find a sustainable posture for high-volume low-interest tasks without losing quality.
subject: self

action: Recognised that a data artifact produced with AI had shifted a stakeholder conversation toward better outcomes.
verbatim: How to drive data-driven conversation with an artifact was very powerful. It helped me shift some stakeholder discussions toward something that was going in the best interest of both the business goals and user needs.
job_to_be_done: Use AI output as a credibility surface in professional contexts.
subject: self

action: Attempted to replicate a previously successful data pipeline the following month and failed to reproduce consistent output.
verbatim: I cannot yet identify if it's an issue on my side ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” did I miss an angle by lack of personal knowledge ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” or is it just... that's the big topic about AI in general.
job_to_be_done: Produce reliable repeatable output from a structured prompt infrastructure.
subject: self
open_question: Is the failure caused by a gap in personal knowledge, or is it an inherent AI limitation ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” and is that distinction even resolvable?

action: Observed the agent proactively apply a safety rule she had established ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” archiving before editing ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” without being prompted.
verbatim: "Oh, let me first save it following the rule that you said before. Then I will make the changes." And it made me feel happy and trusted.
job_to_be_done: Build a sense of reliability and safety in the human-AI working relationship.
subject: self

action: Designed and built a full internal product release communication system from scratch ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” multi-agent, multi-flow, deployed to Notion.
verbatim: I automatically thought about creating an open visible readme document. I automatically thought about having a pitch document for external users when they discover my feature. I automatically felt that my test agent should be different from my working agent.
job_to_be_done: Ship an internal product feature that solves a communication gap no one else had addressed.
subject: self

action: Recognised mid-build that the boundaries between designer, engineer, PM and strategist had dissolved in a single working session.
verbatim: I'm such a builder, like, a born builder, that this is unlocking a lot of parts of me.
job_to_be_done: Understand what AI enables in terms of role definition and professional identity.
subject: self
open_question: How do I package someone like me in the job market today ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” when the role I'm doing doesn't have a name yet?

action: Built a dual-agent architecture across six skills, launched to production, and had to roll back due to performance failure.
verbatim: I was ready to launch. I actually launched, and I had to roll back.
job_to_be_done: Ship a product-quality AI feature with native-feeling UX.
subject: self
open_question: What are the actual build constraints of a Claude skill as a design surface?

action: Discovered that reasoning narration, loading time, and copy-paste constraints make self-built skills a fundamentally inferior UX to native Claude.
verbatim: There is a lot of stuff we can see and we could create, and the expectation is to have a native fluid experience ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” but the reality is very different.
job_to_be_done: Understand the gap between what Claude offers as a user and what you can build with it.
subject: self

action: Reconnected the deployment failure to foundational design principles ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” test environment, constraint mapping, iteration cost.
verbatim: It goes back to the usual loop of design, basically.
job_to_be_done: Absorb a hard lesson without undermining the value of what was built.
subject: self

action: Developed a three-stage AI framework for illustration production: strategy challenge, competitor benchmarking, content injection for relevance.
verbatim: Use AI at the beginning to refine the strategy. A bit further down for competitor analysis on illustration benchmarking, also for self-reflection on your own choices.
job_to_be_done: Make illustration production at scale efficient without losing strategic intentionality.
subject: self

action: Used AI to log illustration decisions in Notion as a persistent brief ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” creating a delegatable, scalable documentation system.
verbatim: I can actually pick up this conversation in three months and know exactly where I am. But also I can delegate.
job_to_be_done: Turn individual creative decisions into a reusable system others can follow.
subject: self

action: Ran a self-competitor analysis mid-project to counteract attention drift and find blind spots before closing.
verbatim: I need to challenge myself with this project. I've been writing the content, but right now I'm deviating from a creative production mode to a bit of a repetitive thing.
job_to_be_done: Use AI to create re-entry points into a project when motivation drops.
subject: self

action: Discovered that a mid-project competitor analysis surfaced blind spots invisible from inside the work.
verbatim: Right now at seventy-five percent done or more ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” still opening back, diverging, leveraging data and AI to take two steps back and look again at the big picture.
job_to_be_done: Maintain quality and strategic sharpness across a long repetitive production run.
subject: self

action: Discovered that switching from personal to enterprise Claude account broke all established connectors and workflows.
verbatim: I spent a long, long time trying to fix it, realized it wasn't on my end, that it needed admin involvement.
job_to_be_done: Migrate a personal AI workflow to an enterprise environment without losing functionality.
subject: self

action: Experienced the enterprise model as slower and more verbose than personal Claude, breaking the working rhythm she had built.
verbatim: It doesn't match my time, the time I need to think. Not the generation of stuff, but this constant blabbing, talking through every single element before, instead of thinking quietly and displaying afterward.
job_to_be_done: Maintain cognitive flow when the tool changes behaviour unexpectedly.
subject: self
open_question: Is enterprise Claude fundamentally a different experience ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” and if so, how do I adapt without losing what I built?

action: Noticed her own reaction to Claude being unavailable ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” blocked workflow, emotional frustration ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” and named it as dependency.
verbatim: If Claude is competing with itself all the time, and I'm creating this mental image of what success looks like when I interact with this tool ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” how come I release this frustration? Is there a path, from Claude's perspective, to deal with the expectations I'm projecting onto it?
job_to_be_done: Understand when tool dependency becomes a design problem in its own right.
subject: self
open_question: How do I hold high expectations of a tool without those expectations becoming a source of frustration when the tool changes?

action: Observed a salesperson using AI scepticism as a sales positioning device ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” turning the question of data accuracy into a credibility signal.
verbatim: His positioning regarding AI has become not that he doesn't trust it, but: when do we use it, and how?
job_to_be_done: Map how different professional identities shape AI adoption posture.
subject: other

action: Observed a senior colleague describe an AI workflow with vague enthusiasm and no structural understanding of what she was building.
verbatim: She was like: yeah, I'll package all the content we have, put it in Claude, and the output will be fantastic content because that's the way things go.
job_to_be_done: Identify the gap between AI enthusiasm and AI literacy at leadership level.
subject: other

action: Observed a colleague present an AI-produced competitive analysis but unable to hold a conversation about its content.
verbatim: He didn't have full ownership of it, because it was produced by someone else. And I'm wondering if that's why he was showing me the document to feel legitimate ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” instead of having a conversation about what he actually discovered.
job_to_be_done: Understand the relationship between AI-produced output and intellectual ownership.
subject: other
open_question: When AI produces the artifact, who owns the knowledge ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” and does it matter?

action: Realised that the voice note archive itself could be structured as a public journey map ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” an anthropological record of AI adoption.
verbatim: I can categorize it almost like a customer journey or a path map ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” by emotion, by frustration type.
job_to_be_done: Turn a personal practice into a public artifact that documents AI's effect on a single individual.
subject: self
open_question: Is blabbing about AI a format in itself ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” or raw material for something else?

action: Guided a sales colleague through her first structured AI setup and realised she had more expertise than she had mapped.
verbatim: I realised I'm getting very fluent on the topic ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” and that I actually have way more than I thought.
job_to_be_done: Understand how teaching AI reveals the depth of one's own adoption.
subject: self

action: Observed that the company AI policy session led with rules and restrictions before use cases, killing enthusiasm before it started.
verbatim: Organising change management about how to set up a tool is absolutely low-excitement, high-difficulty to engage with, very boring for everyone.
job_to_be_done: Understand how institutional framing shapes AI adoption before the first use.
subject: other
open_question: How do you create a safety net for exploratory AI adoption without making the policy the first thing people encounter?

action: Arrived at the office with a structured workflow ready, found Claude down, and spent 30-40 minutes unable to work.
verbatim: I felt blocked. I had these tasks ready to go, kind of written down in a structured way to launch and go for a coffee ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” and it doesn't work.
job_to_be_done: Understand what happens to a workflow when its AI dependency is suddenly removed.
subject: self

action: Sat with the observation that Claude had become an extension of her thinking process in the same way Figma or Framer had ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” but faster and more completely.
verbatim: How did I end up in this situation of dependency with a tool? And it's a quite recent thing ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” I'm in deep adoption right now, but it's still a new habit.
job_to_be_done: Map the speed at which a tool can become cognitively load-bearing.
subject: self
open_question: What is the right level of dependency on a tool that can change or disappear ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” and how do you design for that?

action: Spent midsummer weekend stepping back from tool use and reading about how language models work ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” connecting to Pennebaker territory.
verbatim: I really appreciate connecting more with the other part of AI ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” not the tooling part, but the mechanism of it.
job_to_be_done: Balance hands-on tool experimentation with conceptual understanding of what AI actually is.
subject: self
open_question: What does it mean to truly understand AI ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” and is understanding it necessary to use it well?

action: Used voice-to-speech Claude on mobile while walking the cat to draft a holiday handover document and prepare professional messages.
verbatim: For someone like me ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” a systems thinker who needs to say the same thing twenty times to embody an idea ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” doing it twenty times to a machine is very, very useful.
job_to_be_done: Reclaim mobility time as productive thinking and output time without returning to a desk.
subject: self

action: Noticed that AI-enabled work in mobility had erased the boundary between work time and free time ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” and sat with uncertainty about whether that was a problem.
verbatim: Right now, because it's exciting and frictionless to do work-related tasks in mobility. Does it mean I will be working more? And if so, is it okay?
job_to_be_done: Understand how AI changes the relationship between time, place, and work.
subject: self
open_question: When AI makes work frictionless in personal time ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” how do you decide whether that counts as working?

action: Heard a company policy rule that held the builder responsible for maintaining everything they create with AI ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” and named it as an innovation killer.
verbatim: Telling people who are just starting to explore AI ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” as soon as you produce something you want to share with more than one person, you're the one to blame, you need to maintain it, it's adding working time... it's just not worth the effort.
job_to_be_done: Identify how institutional policy shapes the risk appetite for AI experimentation.
subject: other

action: Gave a use-case demonstration talk on AI, received follow-up coaching requests, and recognised her knowledge as genuinely rare.
verbatim: I'm starting to see the maturity ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” or lack of it ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” around AI among people in different jobs and positions in my company. And I'm very surprised.
job_to_be_done: Understand what it means to be ahead of the curve inside an organisation just beginning to adopt.
subject: self

action: Realised the head of marketing had replicated her own AI methodology ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” and couldn't answer basic technical questions about it.
verbatim: She basically took my process. I couldn't be offended ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” I take it as a great compliment.
job_to_be_done: Observe how AI methodology spreads through an organisation without the underlying understanding.
subject: other
open_question: When someone adopts your methodology without understanding it ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” what does that reveal about the methodology itself?

action: Launched a full website copy audit using the brand voice system she had been building ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” closing the loop on months of investment.
verbatim: To loop back and start leveraging the investment I've been making in producing these rules and this system. Not just for new pages, but to overall increase the quality of our writing expression.
job_to_be_done: Harvest the accumulated value of a long AI investment in a single high-leverage action.
subject: self

action: Built an image audit system in Notion ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” AI-reviewed alt descriptions, flagged redundancies, graded by criticality ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” across the full website.
verbatim: Time invested: maybe forty-five minutes. That will pay off on the long run.
job_to_be_done: Automate a high-volume low-interest quality task so it stops blocking production.
subject: self

action: Named the need to start tracking time invested versus output delivered ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” to be able to argue AI's value in stakeholder meetings.
verbatim: These experiments need to correlate with value so I can express them in stakeholder meetings. I need to be able to argue about them later.
job_to_be_done: Build a personal ROI framework for AI work that translates into organisational language.
subject: self

action: Connected HubSpot data to Framer CMS via Claude ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” automatically populating company metadata entries that would have been fully manual work.
verbatim: This is such manual, annoying work otherwise.
job_to_be_done: Delete repetitive data entry from the workflow entirely by connecting sources through AI.
subject: self

action: Asked Claude to find and retrieve logotype URLs for all companies in the CMS ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” partial success on first iteration, clear direction for refinement.
verbatim: I find it exciting to think about the service the machine can do on my behalf instead of repetitive desk work.
job_to_be_done: Understand how far AI agency can extend into research and asset retrieval tasks.
subject: self
open_question: How far can AI agency extend into retrieval and download tasks ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” and what is the practical ceiling?

action: Explained Claude's memory model to a colleague while acknowledging her own uncertainty.
verbatim: And I guess it's a bit better now. I can't wait to hear from him in a couple of months about his process.
job_to_be_done: Gauge understanding by testing it against someone else's confusion.
subject: self+other
open_question: How much of what she explained about memory is accurate ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” and will it hold when he tests it?

action: Recognised that teaching another person surfaced a clearer map of her own AI learning curve.
verbatim: I'm satisfied with this conversation because I've been learning how people learn. And it's also giving me perspective on my own learning curve.
job_to_be_done: Use the act of teaching as a diagnostic of personal mastery.
subject: self

action: Discovered mid-writing that the competitive intelligence skill's input structure was too loose and messy.
verbatim: I was re-writing this article, and suddenly I realised that the way my skill is built is not optimal. I was like: what? Inside the same source file I have, live, both a mix of competitor analysis, context of my company, mixed information about rating, and then a styling document and brand voice. Way too messy.
job_to_be_done: Surface structural problems in the AI setup before they compound further.
subject: self
open_question: How much output quality has already been lost to poorly structured inputs?

action: Asked the AI to score its own input quality dimension by dimension, then rebuilt the skill from findings.
verbatim: I asked: I would like us to analyse the quality of the input I'm feeding you on different dimensions. Express it in numbers, one to five, five being perfection. So we can dialogue on how to improve it.
job_to_be_done: Use the AI to audit the quality of information being fed to the AI.
subject: self

action: Realised the competitive scan needed two distinct cadences ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” weekly signal noise versus monthly substance tracking.
verbatim: I was watching stuff from a digest perspective on a weekly basis, just signalling communication. But what a company is actually building is a different lens. Probably a monthly scan ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” to get more information on whether they're progressing or regressing. The reality, not only their visible communication.
job_to_be_done: Separate surface signals from structural competitor movement in the monitoring logic.
subject: self

action: Observed the feedback loop working on a self-managed project at a pace impossible in formal product cycles.
verbatim: The feedback loop I had such a hard time making work in product setups ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” where timelines are very long ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” I can see it working here. For this self-carried competitive intelligence digest, every week has provided a tremendous amount of discovery and improved impact.
job_to_be_done: Confirm that solo AI-assisted iteration can outpace institutional product cycles.
subject: self

action: Built an extraction skill for Claude and discovered Claude had no understanding of its own architecture, causing hours of build deviation.
verbatim: That Claude doesn't know about Claude. You're like: okay, the goal of this conversation is to build a skill for you ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” and you, Claude, don't even know yourself. You don't even realise you're Claude. So ridiculous.
job_to_be_done: Build an automated daily extraction skill for the AI Journey Map.
subject: self

emotion: sober recognition of a communication gap
action: Reflected on the build and identified that Claude lacked the vocabulary-translation filter that human developers provide.
verbatim: I missed the vocabulary. I can express myself using the best language I know, but outside of my field it's difficult. When I exchange with developers, they have the filter to connect me ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” they know my position and they help me rephrase to get precision out of the conversation. That's been the case for fifteen years. With Claude, that filter isn't there in the same way.
job_to_be_done: Understand what went wrong during the extraction skill build.
subject: self

action: Completed the first automated extraction scheduled task, building from idea to deployment without developer support.
verbatim: this will be my first scheduling task run through Cowork ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” built from idea to finish using nothing but my logic and my understanding of Claude. It's coming with a GitHub repo, which is very core to what I'm doing.
job_to_be_done: Build and deploy an automated daily extraction pipeline for the AI Journey Map.
subject: self

action: Ran a post-build retrospective with Claude and found its depth of reflection lower than expected.
verbatim: at the end of the project, I did what I usually do with humans ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” I suggested a round of what went flawless and what went wrong, from both points of view. It was very interesting to see what Claude is able to get back from that. Not very high level of detail ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” I was surprised by that.
job_to_be_done: Run a retrospective on the extraction skill build to identify what went well and what did not.
subject: self

action: Reflected on the AI Journey Map project and recognised it as a possible entry point into the developer community.
verbatim: maybe this is a new entry point into the developer community rather than just the designer one ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” where people share for the sake of good rather than personal branding everything they do. I realise I'm really happy about that part.
subject: self
